title: Motorboat Mechanics and Service Technicians
description: Repair and adjust electrical and mechanical equipment of inboard or inboard-outboard boat engines.

tasks.text: Start motors and monitor performance for signs of malfunctioning, such as smoke, excessive vibration, or misfiring.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Document inspection and test results and work performed or to be performed.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Mount motors to boats, and operate boats at various speeds on waterways to conduct operational tests.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Repair engine mechanical equipment, such as power tilts, bilge pumps, or power take-offs.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform routine engine maintenance on motorboats, such as changing oil and filters.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Replace parts, such as gears, magneto points, piston rings, or spark plugs, and reassemble engines.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Idle motors and observe thermometers to determine the effectiveness of cooling systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect and repair or adjust propellers or propeller shafts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Adjust carburetor mixtures, electrical point settings, or timing while motors are running in water-filled test tanks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set starter locks and align and repair steering or throttle controls, using gauges, screwdrivers, or wrenches.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Disassemble and inspect motors to locate defective parts, using mechanic's hand tools and gauges.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Adjust generators and replace faulty wiring, using hand tools and soldering irons.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Repair or rework parts, using machine tools such as lathes, mills, drills, or grinders.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
